rationale: Both reviews are well-grounded and cite concrete file/line evidence, but X's #1 finding (the version=1/2/default cascade silently swallowing failures and falling through to an unpinned kernel) closely matches the ground truth's two strongest live concerns (RuixiangMa and yenuo26 both flag the try/except masking failures as a CI false-pass risk), and X's #3 independently surfaces a still-present variant of the attn_func=None crash class that RuixiangMa raised. Y's findings (test-path coverage gaps, a docstring wording nit, duplication) are technically valid but mostly orthogonal to what the actual PR reviewers cared about, since the SM90-gating and piecewise-crash bugs were already fixed in the shown diff and Y doesn't catch the residual masked-path issue or frame the version-cascade concern as sharply. Both are similarly actionable and precise, but X's findings track the ground truth's substance better.
